welcome.title: Welcome to GrindLab!
welcome.desc: The AI coaching platform that analyzes every decision in your game, identifies your leaks, and builds a personalized study plan so you can grow as a player.

upload.title: Import your hand history
upload.desc: Export a .txt file from PokerStars, GGPoker, ACR or CoinPoker and import it here. The system automatically analyzes every decision, equity, position, ICM, and generates a full report of your session.
upload.hint: Not sure where to find the file? Check the per-site export guide.

train.title: Train with Ghost Table & Sparring
train.desc: The Ghost Table converts your leaks into review drills with adaptive SRS, you only review what you need, when you need it. Sparring has you play your own historical hands, pausing at each decision to evaluate your choice.

ready.title: You're all set!
ready.desc: Import your first tournament for a full diagnosis, or see a sample analysis now.

exportGuide.title: How to export your hands
exportGuide.intro: Export the .txt hand history file from your site and upload it here. We detect the site automatically.

pokerstars.name: PokerStars
pokerstars.where: In the lobby: Settings → Playing History → Hand History → tick 'Save My Hand History' and Apply. Saved by default to C:\Program Files\PokerStars\HandHistory\. Not available on mobile.

ggpoker.name: GGPoker
ggpoker.where: In the client, open PokerCraft (top menu). Pick game type, date range and stakes → Show → select the sessions → Download Hand History. Game Summaries (for the results file) are in the same place. GGPoker does not send them by email.

acr.name: ACR / WPN
acr.where: In the client, click the settings gear (top right) → Hand Histories (left menu) → choose the destination folders for Hand Histories and Tournament Summaries → OK. For prize and ROI, also upload the results file (.ots).

coinpoker.name: CoinPoker
coinpoker.where: In the client: Hand History, export the .txt file.

exportGuide.note: You can upload multiple files at once. The same tournament is merged automatically when the site splits the hand history across files.

sample.sourceTip: GTO Solver evaluation for this spot.
sample.why: With 34% equity against his range, you need 42% to call. You're 8 points short, so calling burns EV. Fold is correct here.
sample.ctaTitle: This is one hand. You have hundreds.
sample.ctaSub: Import your history and see all your leaks.
